food_subgroup: Indian / Tamil — spiced pepper broth / soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI for Milagu Water / Rasam. Mapped to generic soup food product as closest defensible anchor. A more specific South Indian broth class does not exist in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Soups and broths) used as closest defensible category. No specific Rasam or Milagu Water entry exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Milagu Water / Rasam. Not a clinical entity requiring SNOMED mapping.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Milagu Water is approximately 150 ml (about 150 g), served as a side broth alongside rice or as a light starter soup.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Milagu Water (Rasam) is a traditional South Indian pepper broth. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods using the closest published entry for lentil-based South Indian soups and rasam-type preparations. Saturated fat estimated from known fat composition of coconut oil and lentil-based broths. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and water content are not individually reported in ICMR-NIN for this preparation and are set to -1 to indicate data unavailability.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson et al.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for Milagu Water / Rasam. GI of 35 is estimated by analogy to red lentil soup (GI ~21–30) and tamarind-based lentil preparations, adjusted upward slightly for the dilute broth format. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (35 × 12.75) / 100 ≈ 4.5. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content 210 mg per 100 g; potassium 280 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: Milagu Water contains moderate sodium (210 mg/100 g); individuals with hypertension should limit portion size and avoid adding extra salt, while benefiting from the potassium content (280 mg/100 g) which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35), high dietary fibre (2.2 g/100 g), moderate protein (3.8 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Milagu Water has a low estimated GI of approximately 35 and a glycemic load of ~4.5 per 150 ml serving, making it suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without added sugar or high-GI accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.8 g/100 g), high potassium, anti-inflammatory spices (black pepper, turmeric, cumin)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Milagu Water is low in saturated fat and contains anti-inflammatory compounds from black pepper and turmeric; its potassium content supports vascular health, making it a heart-friendly broth when sodium is managed.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anti-inflammatory spices: black pepper (piperine), turmeric (curcumin), ginger
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The piperine in black pepper and curcumin from turmeric in Milagu Water have documented anti-inflammatory properties that may provide symptomatic benefit for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Milagu Water, known in Tamil as மிளகுத் தண்ணீர் (Milagu Thanneer), is a traditional South Indian spiced pepper broth that forms the culinary ancestor of the Anglo-Indian Mulligatawny soup. The name literally translates to 'pepper water' in Tamil. It is prepared by simmering black pepper, tamarind, tomato, red lentils (masoor dal), cumin, mustard seeds, curry leaves, garlic, and turmeric, then tempering with mustard seeds and asafoetida in oil. The result is a thin, tangy, peppery broth with a warming heat from black pepper and a sour depth from tamarind. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 85 kcal, 3.8 g protein, 4.5 g fat, 8.5 g carbohydrate, 2.2 g dietary fibre, and 210 mg sodium. It has a low estimated glycemic index of ~35, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The potassium content (280 mg/100 g) supports blood pressure regulation, relevant for hypertension management. Anti-inflammatory compounds from black pepper (piperine) and turmeric (curcumin) may benefit individuals with inflammatory conditions. Milagu Water is vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. It is traditionally served as a side broth alongside steamed rice, as a digestive after meals, or as a light starter. It is a staple of Tamil Brahmin cuisine and is widely consumed across South India and among the Tamil diaspora in Singapore, Malaysia, and Sri Lanka.
common_user_queries: Is Pepper Water good for diabetes?
South Indian pepper soup health benefits
Is Milagu Water good for blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods using the closest published lentil-based South Indian soup entry. No exact Milagu Water / Rasam entry exists in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central. GI is an analogous estimate from red lentil soup data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Saturated fat estimated from known fat composition of the preparation. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and water content not individually reported in ICMR-NIN for this preparation; set to -1.
notes: G3 NUMERIC HYGIENE — MISSING DATA DOCUMENTATION: monounsaturated_fat_g is set to -1: detailed monounsaturated fatty acid fractions for Milagu Water / Rasam are not individually reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024; no approved source provides this breakdown for this traditional preparation. polyunsaturated_fat_g is set to -1: polyunsaturated fatty acid fractions for Milagu Water / Rasam are not individually reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024; no approved source provides this breakdown for this traditional preparation. trans_fat_g is set to -1: trans fat content for Milagu Water / Rasam is not individually reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024; this is a traditionally prepared plant-based broth with no industrially hydrogenated ingredients, but a confirmed zero cannot be cited from an approved source. cholesterol_mg is set to -1: cholesterol content for Milagu Water / Rasam is not individually reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024; this is a fully plant-based preparation and cholesterol is expected to be negligible, but a confirmed zero cannot be cited from an approved source. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for Milagu Water / Rasam is not individually reported in ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024 for this specific preparation. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero): this is a fully plant-based preparation with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally zero. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 (naturally zero): no vitamin D-containing ingredients are present in this preparation; vitamin D is naturally zero. Saturated fat (1.8 g/100 g) is estimated from the known fat profile of coconut oil and lentil-based preparations as documented in ICMR-NIN. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate based on red lentil soup GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Milagu Water exists in the approved GI literature. The input description conflates Milagu Water (Rasam) with Mulligatawny soup; these are distinct dishes. Milagu Water is the original South Indian pepper broth; Mulligatawny is an Anglo-Indian adaptation. This record covers the traditional South Indian Milagu Water / Rasam preparation. Portions in the input contained duplicate entries; deduplicated to two distinct serving sizes (150 ml and 250 ml). The input listed ICMR-NIN as source; this is an approved primary source for Indian dishes and has been retained.

Iteration 2: Updated ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'; consolidated notes field to document -1 and 0.0 values.
